Would someone please explain the storm surge flooding ? When they say 9 feet above the ground is that based off of ground at sea level or 9 feet above ALL ground no matter what the elevation is? Thank you
I think it’s above the actual ground level - so our house is at 8 ft above sea level but the storm surge viewer is showing surge at 3+ feet in our area.
sea level, which fluctuates with the tide. (Depends on time of day and location)
it’s an ADDITIONAL to the tide (high or low). If the tide is at 2 feet then add the 9 feet to get 11 feet
9’ above sea level, but if you have ponds or small lakes around, look for rainfall height rather than surge height
normal water level
it's above sea level

The surge (in feet) that meteorologists talk about is based right where the land starts on the water (sea level). In this case, on the Gulf of Mexico next to the water (zero foot mean sea level). Let's say the surge is predicted to be 9 feet above mean sea level. If your house is right on the Gulf, you are in deep doo doo (unless the house is raised above 9 feet). If your house is 10 miles inland AND at 5 feet mean sea level elevation that does not mean with 100% certainty that you will see another 4 foot surge (5 feet + 4 feet = 9 feet) at your house because the surge needs to travel 10 miles inland and there are natural and man made obstacles (trees, buildings, etc..). However, the problem is because the ground is currently very saturated the surge might travel further inland X feet because the ground cannot absorb it. Also, if you live near a pond or river that "adds" to the problem of water surge. I hope I answered your question and did not confuse you.
above sea level
